files.patch:
@@ -1,81 +1,30 @@
-# Beads - AI-Native Issue Tracking
+# Beads (beads_rust) — Repo-Local Issue Tracking
 
-Welcome to Beads! This repository uses **Beads** for issue tracking - a modern, AI-native tool designed to live directly in your codebase alongside your code.
+This repo uses `br` (beads_rust) for issue tracking. Issues live in `.beads/`:
 
-## What is Beads?
-
-Beads is issue tracking that lives in your repo, making it perfect for AI coding agents and developers who want their issues close to their code. No web UI required - everything works through the CLI and integrates seamlessly with git.
-
-**Learn more:** [github.com/steveyegge/beads](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads)
+- `.beads/beads.db` (SQLite; source of truth)
+- `.beads/issues.jsonl` (git-friendly export)
 
 ## Quick Start
 
-### Essential Commands
-
 ```bash
-# Create new issues
-bd create "Add user authentication"
+# Create
+br create "Add user authentication" -t task -p 2
 
-# View all issues
-bd list
+# List / show
+br list
+br show bd-123
 
-# View issue details
-bd show <issue-id>
+# Work state
+br update bd-123 --status in_progress
+br close bd-123 --reason "Completed"
 
-# Update issue status
-bd update <issue-id> --status in_progress
-bd update <issue-id> --status done
-
-# Sync with git remote
-bd sync
+# Sync (explicit)
+br sync --flush-only   # DB -> issues.jsonl (before git add/commit)
+br sync --import-only  # issues.jsonl -> DB (after git pull)
 ```
 
-### Working with Issues
-
-Issues in Beads are:
-- **Git-native**: Stored in `.beads/issues.jsonl` and synced like code
-- **AI-friendly**: CLI-first design works perfectly with AI coding agents
-- **Branch-aware**: Issues can follow your branch workflow
-- **Always in sync**: Auto-syncs with your commits
-
-## Why Beads?
-
-✨ **AI-Native Design**
-- Built specifically for AI-assisted development workflows
-- CLI-first interface works seamlessly with AI coding agents
-- No context switching to web UIs
-
-🚀 **Developer Focused**
-- Issues live in your repo, right next to your code
-- Works offline, syncs when you push
-- Fast, lightweight, and stays out of your way
-
-🔧 **Git Integration**
-- Automatic sync with git commits
-- Branch-aware issue tracking
-- Intelligent JSONL merge resolution
-
-## Get Started with Beads
-
-Try Beads in your own projects:
-
-```bash
-# Install Beads
-curl -sSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
-
-# Initialize in your repo
-bd init
-
-# Create your first issue
-bd create "Try out Beads"
-```
-
-## Learn More
-
-- **Documentation**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/docs](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/docs)
-- **Quick Start Guide**: Run `bd quickstart`
-- **Examples**: [github.com/steveyegge/beads/examples](https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/tree/main/examples)
-
----
+## Notes for Agents
 
-*Beads: Issue tracking that moves at the speed of thought* ⚡
+- Prefer `--json` output when scripting (`br list --json`, `br ready --json`).
+- Use `bv --robot-triage` to pick work (never run bare `bv`).

@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
 # Beads Configuration File
-# This file configures default behavior for all bd commands in this repository
-# All settings can also be set via environment variables (BD_* prefix)
+# This file configures default behavior for all br (beads_rust) commands in this repository
+# All settings can also be set via environment variables (BEADS_* prefix)
 # or overridden with command-line flags
 
-# Issue prefix for this repository (used by bd init)
-# If not set, bd init will auto-detect from directory name
+# Issue prefix for this repository (used by br init)
+# If not set, br init will auto-detect from directory name
 # Example: issue-prefix: "myproject" creates issues like "myproject-1", "myproject-2", etc.
 # issue-prefix: ""
 
 # Use no-db mode: load from JSONL, no SQLite, write back after each command
-# When true, bd will use .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth
+# When true, br will use .beads/issues.jsonl as the source of truth
 # instead of SQLite database
 # no-db: false
 
@@ -25,7 +25,7 @@
 # Enable JSON output by default
 # json: false
 
-# Default actor for audit trails (overridden by BD_ACTOR or --actor)
+# Default actor for audit trails (overridden by BEADS_ACTOR or --actor)
 # actor: ""
 
 # Path to database (overridden by BEADS_DB or --db)
@@ -37,22 +37,22 @@
 # Debounce interval for auto-flush (can also use BEADS_FLUSH_DEBOUNCE)
 # flush-debounce: "5s"
 
-# Git branch for beads commits (bd sync will commit to this branch)
+# Git branch for beads commits (br sync will commit to this branch)
 # IMPORTANT: Set this for team projects so all clones use the same sync branch.
 # This setting persists across clones (unlike database config which is gitignored).
 # Can also use BEADS_SYNC_BRANCH env var for local override.
-# If not set, bd sync will require you to run 'bd config set sync.branch <branch>'.
+# If not set, br sync will require you to run 'br config set sync.branch <branch>'.
 # sync-branch: "beads-sync"
 
-# Multi-repo configuration (experimental - bd-307)
+# Multi-repo configuration (experimental)
 # Allows hydrating from multiple repositories and routing writes to the correct JSONL
 # repos:
 #   primary: "."  # Primary repo (where this database lives)
 #   additional:   # Additional repos to hydrate from (read-only)
 #     - ~/beads-planning  # Personal planning repo
 #     - ~/work-planning   # Work planning repo
 
-# Integration settings (access with 'bd config get/set')
+# Integration settings (access with 'br config get/set')
 # These are stored in the database, not in this file:
 # - jira.url
 # - jira.project

 {"id":"bd-11gq","title":"Tree node markup parsing tests","description":"## Problem
Tree nodes may have similar markup parsing issues. Need explicit tests to verify and document behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Tree node labels with markup - verify if parsed
- [ ] Test Tree with styled branches
- [ ] Verify ANSI codes present in rendered output (if markup should work)
- [ ] Verify raw markup tags NOT present in rendered output
- [ ] Document the expected API clearly

## Implementation Notes
- Check Tree implementation for markup handling
- Add explicit tests to lock down current behavior
- May need Tree::add_node_markup() if markup isn't supported

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/tree_tests.rs  
- src/renderables/tree.rs (if fix needed)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:52.753560616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526581194Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T08:00:16.526523125Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}

- Provide a Pretty renderable for Rust values (Debug) with optional syntax highlighting
- Provide an inspect-like helper that prints type info + fields when possible

- Unit tests cover nested structs/collections
- Document Rust-idiomatic differences vs Python inspect

 {"id":"bd-1ub","title":"Close Rich port gaps: parity, conformance, missing features","description":"Goal: close every remaining gap in the Python Rich -> rich_rust port by (1) establishing authoritative parity documentation, (2) adding true conformance tests against Python Rich output, and (3) implementing currently-missing features (Live, Layout, logging handler, HTML/SVG export) or explicitly documenting scope decisions.\n\nWhy: README still lists missing Rich features; spec/docs are out of sync with implemented functionality; conformance tests do not compare against Python Rich. This epic coordinates the remediation so future work does not rely on tribal knowledge.\n\nDefinition of done: FEATURE_PARITY.md exists and is authoritative; RICH_SPEC/README are consistent; conformance fixtures exist and are enforced; each missing feature is either implemented with tests/docs or explicitly excluded with rationale.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:24:50.961255334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:26:09.960421969Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:26:09.960404606Z","close_reason":"All parity docs, conformance harness, and missing features completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Decide whether to implement or permanently exclude. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md, README, and RICH_SPEC accordingly.\n\nAcceptance: clear documented decision; if in-scope, follow-up beads created for spec/impl/tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T00:33:00.781151892Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:21.787501280Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T00:37:21.787080357Z","close_reason":"Decided input widgets are out of scope and documented","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ub.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1ub","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1vqq","title":"Fresh-eyes code exploration + bug audit pass (random modules)","description":"Goal: randomly sample core rendering modules, trace execution flows, and fix any obvious bugs found. Scope: Console/Segment/Text/Style pipeline and one renderable (Table/Panel/Progress). Method: read code paths end-to-end, cross-check invariants (width, newline handling, style transitions), then add targeted fixes/tests. +{"id":"bd-1x5n","title":"Port rich.theme: Theme + named styles (incl. .ini themes)","description":"Implement Python Rich theme support:

- Theme registry mapping style names -> Style
- Console has a theme, resolves named styles in markup and APIs
- Support loading theme from .ini (Python Rich Theme.read) to keep parity

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests for named style resolution + overrides
- Markup tag [warning] uses theme style when defined
- Conformance fixtures include a theme case
- Update docs/spec/parity

Scope:
- Render exceptions/backtraces to segments with syntax highlighting and locals formatting where possible
- Provide Console::print_exception (or Rust-idiomatic equivalent) matching Rich defaults
- Provide renderable Traceback type

Acceptance criteria:
- Deterministic output for a fixed synthetic stack in tests
- Optional feature flag integration if needed (e.g. backtrace)
- Conformance fixture compares against Python Rich for a controlled exception

 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2xc5","title":"Fresh-eyes audit pass: renderables markdown/json/syntax","description":"Random code exploration: deep dive into markdown/json/syntax renderables, trace flow through console/render pipeline, and fix any obvious issues with tests. Ensure adherence to AGENTS.md (manual edits, no deletions).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:36:05.690235412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393505513Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:00:17.393330824Z","close_reason":"Fix list continuation indent to use cell width for bullet/marker; add regression test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-39jb","title":"Update .beads docs/config for br","description":"The checked-in .beads/README.md and .beads/config.yaml still document legacy bd commands/links, but this repo uses beads_rust via br.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update .beads/README.md to reference br commands (create/list/show/update/close/sync --flush-only)
- Update .beads/config.yaml comments/examples to match beads_rust and current env var prefixes
- Keep content short and repo-specific (rich_rust)

+{"id":"bd-3c90","title":"Full Python Rich parity (beyond current core)","description":"Owner request: remove/exhaust all parity gaps vs upstream Python Rich (currently 13.9.4 in conformance env).

Track missing modules/features (theme, emoji codes, pretty/inspect, traceback, prompt/input, status/pager, advanced progress, etc), expand conformance fixtures, and update RICH_SPEC/FEATURE_PARITY accordingly.

Definition of done:
- All Python Rich public modules/features have Rust equivalents or documented Rust-idiomatic equivalents
- Conformance coverage expanded to cover new features and matches Python outputs where applicable
- README + docs reflect full parity

Scope: terminal detection (env var handling and color system selection) + markup parsing edge cases. Add regression tests where needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T15:22:22.322303363Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T15:30:20.942891466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T15:30:20.942871398Z","close_reason":"Fix: force_terminal now recomputes detected color system when no explicit color_system set","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
+{"id":"bd-eb17","title":"Port rich.prompt/status/pager equivalents (interactive helpers)","description":"Implement the interactive helper layer from Python Rich.

Scope (Rust-idiomatic):
- Prompt: styled input with validation and defaults
- Status: spinner + message context manager
- Pager: send output through pager when available

Acceptance criteria:
- Cross-platform behavior (Unix + Windows) behind crossterm

 {"id":"rich_rust-01n","title":"Validate Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13","description":"## Objective
Verify the text wrapping implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 13 exactly.

## Background
Text wrapping is critical for responsive terminal output. The algorithm must handle word boundaries, long words, wide characters, and styled text spans correctly.

## Validation Checklist

### 13.1 Word Boundary Detection
- [ ] Verify words split on whitespace
- [ ] Verify hyphenated words treated as single unit (or split at hyphen)
- [ ] Verify CJK characters can wrap between any characters

### 13.2 Wrap Algorithm
- [ ] Verify words fit: add to current line
- [ ] Verify word too long: break at width (fold mode)
- [ ] Verify line break: start new line, preserve indent
- [ ] Verify whitespace at line break: consumed (not carried)

### 13.3 Overflow Methods
- [ ] Verify Fold: break anywhere when word > width
- [ ] Verify Crop: truncate at width
- [ ] Verify Ellipsis: truncate at width-1 and add ellipsis

### 13.4 Style Preservation
- [ ] Verify spans are split correctly across line breaks
- [ ] Verify style continues on wrapped lines
- [ ] Verify partial spans at wrap point

### 13.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test wrapping with wide characters (2-cell)
- [ ] Test wrapping with zero-width characters
- [ ] Test wrapping at exactly width boundary
- [ ] Test no_wrap flag
- [ ] Test empty text
- [ ] Test single long word

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (wrap methods)
- src/cells.rs (width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 13","acceptance_criteria":"All wrap scenarios work correctly. Styles preserved across line breaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:19:12.644260470Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:44:58.001495493Z","close_reason":"Validated Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13. All 41 E2E tests and 17 unit tests pass. Implementation correctly handles: word boundary detection via character iteration, wrap algorithm with proper line division, all 4 overflow methods (Fold, Crop, Ellipsis, Ignore), style preservation through slice()/divide(), and edge cases (empty text, whitespace, zero width, wide chars). No gaps found.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-01n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:35:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -562,71 +562,20 @@ Treat cass as a way to avoid re-solving problems other agents already handled.
 
 ## Beads Workflow Integration
 
-This project uses [beads_rust](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/beads_rust) for issue tracking. Issues are stored in `.beads/` and tracked in git.
+This repo uses `br` (beads_rust) for issue tracking. Issues live in `.beads/`.
 
-**Note:** `br` is non-invasive and never executes git commands. You must manually add, commit, and push `.beads/` changes.
-
-**SQLite/WAL Caution:** br uses SQLite with WAL mode. Always run `br sync --flush-only` before git operations to ensure `.beads/` files are consistent.
-
-### Essential Commands
+- Use the Beads issue id (e.g., `bd-123`) as Agent Mail `thread_id` and file-reservation `reason`.
+- Use `bv --robot-triage` (never bare `bv`) to pick work.
+- Run `br sync --flush-only` before any git operations so `.beads/` files are consistent.
 
 ```bash
-# View issues (launches TUI - avoid in automated sessions)
-bv
-
-# CLI commands for agents (use these instead)
-br ready              # Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
-br list --status=open # All open issues
-br show <id>          # Full issue details with dependencies
-br create --title="..." --type=task --priority=2
-br update <id> --status=in_progress
-br close <id> --reason="Completed"
-br close <id1> <id2>  # Close multiple issues at once
-br sync --flush-only  # Export to JSONL (then manually git add/commit/push)
+bv --robot-triage
+br ready --json
+br update bd-123 --status in_progress
+br close bd-123 --reason "Completed"
+br sync --flush-only
 ```
 
-### Workflow Pattern
-
-1. **Start**: Run `br ready` to find actionable work
-2. **Claim**: Use `br update <id> --status=in_progress`
-3. **Work**: Implement the task
-4. **Complete**: Use `br close <id>`
-5. **Sync**: Always run sync workflow at session end:
-   ```bash
-   br sync --flush-only
-   git add .beads/
-   git commit -m "Sync beads"
-   git push
-   ```
-
-### Key Concepts
-
-- **Dependencies**: Issues can block other issues. `br ready` shows only unblocked work.
-- **Priority**: P0=critical, P1=high, P2=medium, P3=low, P4=backlog (use numbers, not words)
-- **Types**: task, bug, feature, epic, question, docs
-- **Blocking**: `br dep add <issue> <depends-on>` to add dependencies
-
-### Session Protocol
-
-**Before ending any session, run this checklist:**
-
-```bash
-git status              # Check what changed
-git add <files>         # Stage code changes
-br sync --flush-only    # Export beads to JSONL
-git add .beads/         # Stage beads changes
-git commit -m "..."     # Commit all changes
-git push                # Push to remote
-```
-
-### Best Practices
-
-- Check `br ready` at session start to find available work
-- Update status as you work (in_progress -> closed)
-- Create new issues with `br create` when you discover tasks
-- Use descriptive titles and set appropriate priority/type
-- Always run sync workflow before ending session
-
